channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Artemis II crew to end record-setting mission with Pacific Ocean splashdown - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Artemis II crew to end record-setting mission with Pacific Ocean splashdown  The Guardian

item.description:

item.title: Oldest octopus fossil found to not be an octopus - Ars Technica
item.description:

  4. 'Oldest octopus' fossil is no octopus at all, scans reveal  Phys.org

item.title: 'The comet went in, but only a cloud of debris came out': Watch bright Comet MAPS get torn apart by the sun - Live Science

item.title: Female mice grow testes after this single DNA tweak - Nature
item.description:
1. Female mice grow testes after this single DNA tweak  Nature
  2. A single-nucleotide enhancer mutation overrides chromosomal sex to drive XX male development  Nature
  3. With a switch of a letter in DNA ‘dark matter,’ Israeli scientists change sex of mouse  The Times of Israel
  4. Changing 'just one DNA letter' in female mice triggers growth of male genitalia  Live Science
  5. One DNA letter can trigger complete sex reversal, Bar-Ilan University study finds  EurekAlert!

item.title: A 289-Million-Year-Old Mummified Reptile From An Oklahoma Cave Shows How We Breathe The Way We Do - IFLScience
item.description:

  4. Permian Fossil is Earliest Evidence of Rib-Powered Breathing  Sci.News

item.title: 'We all pretty much broke down right there': Inside the Artemis 2 astronauts' emotional moment near the moon - Space

item.title: Orion helium leak no threat to Artemis II reentry but will require redesign - Ars Technica
item.description:
1. Orion helium leak no threat to Artemis II reentry but will require redesign  Ars Technica
  2. Artemis II mission update: NASA cancels final manual piloting test of Orion capsule  starlust.org
  3. Orion’s Helium Leak Won’t Endanger Artemis II Crew, but NASA Needs New Valves  Technology Org
  4. Artemis II Is About to Splash Down. There's a Helium Leak That's 10 Times Worse Than Expected. NASA Says It's Fine.  19FortyFive
  5. Will the helium leak in Orion affect Artemis II's re-entry? Here's what NASA says  starlust.org
